text: A nurse reminds a client to wash hands before eating to prevent disease transmission. Which mode of transmission is primarily interrupted?
rationale: Hand hygiene primarily interrupts direct contact transmission and is one of the most effective infection prevention measures.

text: A client with pulmonary tuberculosis is admitted to the hospital. Which precaution should the nurse implement?
rationale: Tuberculosis is spread through airborne droplet nuclei and requires airborne precautions.

text: A child develops malaria after multiple mosquito bites. Which type of disease transmission occurred?
rationale: Malaria is transmitted by the bite of an infected female Anopheles mosquito which acts as a vector.

text: A nurse educates a community about completing prescribed antibiotics. Why is this important?
rationale: Completing the full antibiotic course helps eliminate bacteria and reduces the development of resistant organisms.

text: A nurse is caring for a client with influenza. Which route most commonly spreads this infection?
rationale: Influenza is primarily transmitted through respiratory droplets produced during coughing or sneezing.

text: A client is diagnosed with athlete's foot. Which type of microorganism causes this condition?
rationale: Athlete's foot is a superficial fungal infection caused by dermatophytes.

text: A nurse explains that HIV attacks specific immune cells. Which cells are primarily affected?
rationale: HIV primarily infects and destroys CD4 T lymphocytes leading to immune deficiency.

text: A client develops diarrhea after drinking contaminated water while traveling. Which organism commonly causes amoebic dysentery?
rationale: Entamoeba histolytica is a protozoan parasite transmitted through contaminated food or water.

text: A nurse teaches a client about vaccines. What is the primary purpose of vaccination?
rationale: Vaccines stimulate the immune system to develop protection before exposure to disease.

text: A client complains of intense itching at night and is diagnosed with scabies. What is the causative organism?
rationale: Scabies is caused by the mite Sarcoptes scabiei which burrows into the skin.

text: A client has ringworm on the arm. Which statement by the client indicates understanding?
option_a: It is caused by a worm
option_c: It is caused by a virus
option_d: It is caused by poor circulation
rationale: Ringworm is a fungal infection despite its misleading name.

text: A client is diagnosed with ascariasis after eating contaminated vegetables. Which type of parasite causes this disease?
rationale: Ascaris lumbricoides is a helminth or roundworm transmitted by ingestion of infective eggs.

text: A nurse removes gloves after caring for an infected client. What should the nurse do next?
rationale: Hand hygiene is required immediately after glove removal because gloves are not a substitute for handwashing.

text: A hospitalized client develops oral thrush after prolonged antibiotic therapy. Which organism most likely caused the infection?
rationale: Candida albicans is an opportunistic fungus that commonly causes oral thrush after antibiotic use.

text: A client with typhoid fever is instructed to wash hands after using the toilet. What is the main reason?
rationale: Typhoid fever spreads through the fecal-oral route making hand hygiene essential.

text: A client develops fever every 48 hours after returning from a malaria-endemic area. Which parasite is the likely cause?
rationale: Malaria is caused by Plasmodium parasites transmitted by infected mosquitoes.

text: A nurse explains why sterilization is required before surgery. What is the primary purpose?
rationale: Sterilization destroys all forms of microbial life including bacterial spores.

text: A client asks why viral infections are not usually treated with antibiotics. What is the best response?
rationale: Antibiotics target bacteria and do not kill viruses.

text: A food handler is taught proper food storage to prevent bacterial growth. Which practice is most effective?
option_a: Leave cooked food at room temperature
option_c: Store raw meat above cooked food
rationale: Prompt refrigeration slows bacterial growth and reduces the risk of foodborne illness.
